positional_bias.note: Positions re-derived from quiz.gift match sidecar exactly. All 4 positions used; max_share 0.333 <= 0.40.

weakest_questions.distractor: Obtaining a computer science degree before attempting to write any Python code in a professional or educational context.
weakest_questions.note: Low plausibility as a real beginner misconception; no degree gate was implied in the corpus. Not absurd but weakest distractor in the set. Carried over from cycle 1; below threshold for a fail.

soft_flags.lo: LO4 — Connect Python's role to prior concepts already known: flowcharts, abstraction, and specification writing from weeks 1–10.
soft_flags.issue: q5 tests 'interpreted language / line-by-line execution' semantics, which maps cleanly to LO5. The LO4 claim (connection to flowcharts, abstraction, specification writing from M1) is tenuous — no question stem or answer option references flowcharts, pseudocode, decomposition, or M1 prior work. Coverage accepted via sidecar assertion; flagged as a carry-over from cycle 1 for builder attention in a future revision.

beginner_fairness.note: All acronyms (API, SDK, PyPI) are spelled out inline. All stems are answerable from this topic alone.

verdict: Pass — all checks clear on cycle 2. Length bias resolved: flagged_count dropped from 5 to 0 (longest_correct_rate 0.0, well below 0.40 threshold). Positional bias unchanged and passing (max_share 0.333). Distractor plausibility passing (absurd_rate 0.0; one weak but not absurd distractor in q6 noted). All 6 LOs covered; LO4 soft flag carried over — q5 blends LO4/LO5 by assertion, accepted by design. Beginner fairness passing.
